metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add a plan step and AC that exercises pytest-split (e.g. specifying what command uses it and what outcome it must produce), or remove it from this ticket's scope and note it as a follow-on dependency.
findings.finding: pytest-split is listed as a dep to add to [dev] and pin exactly, but no plan step, workflow command, or acceptance criterion uses or exercises it. An executor has no guidance on what pytest-split is for in this change, whether any AC is satisfied by its presence, or whether it belongs in this ticket at all.
findings.location: What section — first bullet ('Add pytest-xdist and pytest-split…'); Acceptance Criteria AC1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add an AC / CI step that exercises pytest-split (e.g. a split-run smoke test), or remove pytest-split from this ticket's scope and defer it to a follow-on that actually uses it.
findings.finding: pytest-split is added to [dev] deps but no AC, CI command, or workflow step actually uses it. The title promises only xdist parallelization and the coverage fix; adding an unused dependency constitutes minor scope beyond the stated intent with no end-state-observable proof.
findings.location: What section — '[dev] extra in pyproject.toml' / AC line 1: 'pytest-xdist + pytest-split added to [dev], pinned exactly.'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove pytest-split from the [dev] dep change in this ticket. Open a follow-on ticket to introduce --splits/--group parallelism if desired; that ticket can pin and validate pytest-split with a real AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove `pytest-split` from the `[dev]` dep addition (AC line 1 / What section) until a concrete call-site or AC that exercises it exists; it has zero invocations in the plan's own CI commands and violates YAGNI.
findings.finding: pytest-split is added to [dev] as a pinned dependency but has zero call-sites anywhere in the codebase (no --splits, --group, --store-durations, or any pytest-split invocation in gerrit-verify.yaml, test.yml, Makefile, or test files), and no AC or Scope entry exercises it. This violates YAGNI and the Rule-of-Three (0 of ≥3 required call-sites), and constitutes config-surface proliferation in [dev] — the dep must be pinned, maintained, and audited by pip-audit with no delivered capability.
findings.location: What section / AC line 1: 'Add `pytest-xdist` and `pytest-split` to the `[dev]` extra in `pyproject.toml`, pinned exactly'
findings.suggested_fix: Drop `pytest-split` from the pinned additions entirely. If future work needs duration-based job splitting across matrix cells, add it in that story when the --splits/--group invocation is also specified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either remove `pytest-split` from the dep list (if it has no role in this ticket) or add an AC and workflow step that actually uses it (e.g. `--splits`/`--group` for shard-based parallelism), with a rationale for why it belongs in this ticket.
findings.finding: `pytest-split` is added to `[dev]` deps in `pyproject.toml` but is never referenced in any workflow command, AC, or step in the plan — it has no described purpose within the ticket's scope.
findings.location: Scope section / What section: '`pytest-split` to the `[dev]` extra in `pyproject.toml`, pinned exactly'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove `pytest-split` from the `[dev]` extra addition in this ticket; open a follow-on ticket for shard-based test splitting if that is the intent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove `pytest-split` from the `[dev]` extra unless a specific AC or CI command in this plan uses it; if it is intended for a follow-on story, track it there instead.
findings.finding: `pytest-split` is added to `[dev]` as a pinned dependency, but no CI command, workflow step, or acceptance criterion in the plan actually uses it (no `--splits`, `--group`, `--store-durations`, or any pytest-split invocation appears in the scope). The plan's Why section mentions it was 'used ad hoc in experiments only'. Pinning an unused library to the production dev toolchain adds supply-chain surface area and version-churn cost with no mechanistic benefit for this plan's stated goal.
findings.location: What section ('Add pytest-xdist and pytest-split to the [dev] extra') + AC bullet 1 + Scope
findings.suggested_fix: Drop `pytest-split` from the `[dev]` addition. If a future story adds duration-based splitting, add it then with a defined call-site.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either remove `pytest-split` from this leaf's scope (and AC-1) since the parent epic does not include it, or update the parent epic to explicitly scope pytest-split in before re-reviewing this leaf.
findings.finding: The leaf adds `pytest-split` as a pinned [dev] dependency (AC-1 explicitly requires it), but the parent epic's plan contains no mention of pytest-split anywhere — not in its Scope, Success Criteria, Acceptance Criteria, or body. This is a deliverable that lies outside the parent's declared scope.
findings.location: Leaf AC-1 / Leaf What (first bullet) / parent epic ce0e-f618-a2a4-4ee4 Scope
findings.suggested_fix: Remove `pytest-split` from AC-1 and the What bullet. If pytest-split is needed for a future shard-based splitting story, it should be added to the parent epic's Scope IN first, then carried down to the appropriate child. Alternatively, if it is genuinely required by this story, add it to the parent's Scope before re-reviewing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either document the specific pytest-split capability and invocation the plan relies on (e.g., `--store-durations` for a future duration-based split story), or remove `pytest-split` from AC-1 and `[dev]` deps until a concrete use-site exists.
findings.finding: pytest-split is added to [dev] deps as a pinned dependency in AC-1, but no plan section, CI command, workflow step, or acceptance criterion specifies what capability of pytest-split is being used or why it is needed. pytest-split (a duration-based test-splitting library for distributing tests across parallel CI jobs) is distinct from pytest-xdist (in-process parallelization); the xdist parallelization goal of this plan does not inherently require pytest-split. The plan asserts by inclusion that pytest-split is needed, but this capability claim is unverified and ungrounded.
findings.location: What section / AC-1: 'Add `pytest-xdist` and `pytest-split` to the `[dev]` extra in `pyproject.toml`, pinned exactly'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove `pytest-split` from AC-1 and the `[dev]` extra. If duration-based splitting across multiple CI runners is a future goal, track it as a separate story with an explicit invocation spec.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add a concrete plan step that invokes a pytest-split feature (`--store-durations`/`--splits`/`--group`) and verify that flag exists in the installed version, or remove `pytest-split` from AC-1 and the `[dev]` dep list (defer it to the follow-on story that will use it).
findings.finding: `pytest-split` is listed as a pinned `[dev]` dependency in AC-1 and the 'What' section, but no plan step, CI command, workflow YAML change, or AC actually invokes any pytest-split feature (no `--splits`, `--group`, `--store-durations`, or `--durations-path`). The integration surface is entirely unspecified, making this an unverified integration: its capability is not matched to any described usage in this change.
findings.location: What section ('Add `pytest-xdist` and `pytest-split` to the `[dev]` extra'); AC-1 ('`pytest-xdist` + `pytest-split` added to `[dev]`, pinned exactly')
findings.suggested_fix: Remove `pytest-split` from AC-1 and the `[dev]` addition in the 'What' section; add it back in a follow-on story (e.g. duration-store / split-by-duration) when its concrete invocation surface is designed. This keeps the supply-chain footprint of this change to only what is actually used.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the 'serial OR xdist_group-confined' ambiguity for live tests by stating a single preferred approach (e.g. 'keep live tests in a dedicated serial step: `pytest -m live -n0`') so the executor has exactly one implementation path.
findings.finding: The plan says live tests should be 'kept serial OR confine to one worker via @pytest.mark.xdist_group + --dist loadgroup', leaving the implementation choice to the executor. The same 'or' appears verbatim in AC4. This is a minor defaultable gap: without a stated default, an agent may pick the cheaper option (serial) while the author may intend xdist_group confinement (or vice versa), leading to a workflow command mismatch.
findings.location: What section — live-test paragraph; Acceptance Criteria AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the 'or' with: 'Keep live tests in a dedicated serial invocation: `pytest -m "integration and live" -n0 --dist no`; do NOT mark individual test functions with xdist_group in this iteration.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge and document (in What + docs/coverage.md) that `parallel = true` affects ALL `pytest --cov` runs (not just xdist), specify that `coverage combine` is required before `coverage report` in serial runs (or that pytest-cov handles it automatically), and add a one-line rollback path (revert the `pyproject.toml` hunk) in case downstream consumers break.
findings.finding: Adding `parallel = true` to `[tool.coverage.run]` changes behavior for ALL coverage runs — not just xdist — yet the plan only discusses this setting's effect in the xdist context. On a plain serial `pytest --cov` run (e.g., local developer, a future non-xdist CI job), `parallel = true` causes coverage to write `.coverage.<hostname>.<pid>.<rand>` instead of `.coverage`, so a bare `coverage report` or `coverage html` after the run silently reports 0 % (or errors) unless `coverage combine` is run first. The plan neither acknowledges this side-effect for serial consumers nor provides a rollback path (e.g., revert `pyproject.toml`) or a compensating note in the docs update.
findings.location: What section — 'FIX COVERAGE UNDER XDIST' paragraph; AC line 2; docs/coverage.md deliverable
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add a note: 'Note: `parallel = true` applies to all coverage runs; pytest-cov automatically calls `coverage combine` so serial runs are unaffected when using the pytest-cov plugin. For bare `coverage report` invocations (scripts, Makefiles), a `coverage combine` step must precede the report.' Also add this to the docs/coverage.md update, and record the rollback as: revert the `[tool.coverage.run] parallel = true` line and drop the `-n` flag from the affected workflow step.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a note on the `@pytest.mark.xdist_group` requirement for live integration tests to a discoverable developer doc (e.g., CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, or a new `docs/testing.md` section) so the contract is enforced for future contributors.
findings.finding: The `@pytest.mark.xdist_group` pattern for live integration tests is a new test-authoring contract — any future live test that omits this marker will silently re-introduce parallel flake — but the plan names no documentation home (CLAUDE.md, CONTRIBUTING, test-writing guide, or similar) for this rule.
findings.location: What section: 'Live tests … keep them serial or confine to one worker via `@pytest.mark.xdist_group` + `--dist loadgroup`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short section to CLAUDE.md or CONTRIBUTING.md: 'Any test touching live external state (marked `[integration, live]`) must carry `@pytest.mark.xdist_group("live")` to prevent parallel flake under xdist. See `docs/coverage.md` for the full xdist setup.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the hardcoded `-n4`/`-n3` with a workflow step that captures `nproc` (or `sysctl -n hw.logicalcpu` on macOS) into an env var and passes it to pytest (`-n ${{ env.PYTEST_WORKERS }}`), or at minimum document in-repo (e.g., a comment in the workflow) the expected vCPU count and a runbook for updating it.
findings.finding: The worker counts `-n4` (ubuntu-latest) and `-n3` (macos arm) are hardcoded in workflow YAML and tied to GitHub-Actions runner tier specs, which are not under the project's control. The plan acknowledges this risk with an `nproc` echo but does not derive `-n` dynamically from that echo, leaving a static threshold that will silently under-parallelize (or mis-parallelize) if runner specs change.
findings.location: What section: 'pin `-n4` (ubuntu-latest, 4 vCPU) / `-n3` (macos arm, 3 vCPU), NOT `-n auto`'; Scope: 'Verify runner vCPU with an in-workflow `nproc` echo (belt-and-suspenders) before pinning N'
findings.suggested_fix: In the workflow, add a step: `- run: echo "PYTEST_WORKERS=$(nproc)" >> $GITHUB_ENV` and change the pytest invocation to `-n ${{ env.PYTEST_WORKERS }} --dist worksteal`. This eliminates the static threshold while avoiding local over-subscription (CI always has a controlled core count).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document (in `docs/coverage.md` and/or a comment in `pyproject.toml`) that `parallel = true` requires `coverage combine` before `coverage report` for serial (non-xdist) local runs, and consider providing a `make coverage` / `tox` target that runs combine automatically so developers aren't silently broken.
findings.finding: `parallel = true` in `[tool.coverage.run]` is a global setting that affects ALL coverage runs, not only xdist CI runs. Local developers running `pytest --cov` serially will now produce `.coverage.<PID>` files instead of `.coverage`, silently breaking their local coverage reports unless they separately run `coverage combine`. The plan enumerates no consumers of this config change beyond the CI workflows.
findings.location: pyproject.toml — `[tool.coverage.run] parallel = true`; also Scope section and AC bullet 2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `[tool.coverage.report]` or Makefile/tox target that always runs `coverage combine` before `coverage report`. Alternatively, note in `docs/coverage.md` that local serial runs need `coverage combine && coverage report` (not just `coverage report`) when `parallel = true` is set.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit to ONE live-test isolation strategy in the plan (e.g., always run live tests in a dedicated serial step with no `-n` flag, OR enforce `@pytest.mark.xdist_group('live')` on all live-test files and use `--dist loadgroup` in the integration command) and remove the ambiguous 'or'.
findings.finding: The live-test xdist isolation strategy is stated as 'keep them serial OR confine to one worker via `@pytest.mark.xdist_group` + `--dist loadgroup`' without committing to either approach. For CI environments where Jira credentials ARE present, this ambiguity leaves the implementation undefined, risking eventual-consistency flakiness if the wrong branch is chosen.
findings.location: What section — 'Live tests … keep them serial or confine to one worker via `@pytest.mark.xdist_group` + `--dist loadgroup`'; AC bullet 4
findings.suggested_fix: Prefer the explicit serial step: add a separate CI step `pytest -m 'integration and live' --no-header` (no `-n`) after the parallel non-live integration step. This is simpler, requires no marker changes, and eliminates the race entirely.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's coverage figure from '~80%' to '77%' to match `docs/coverage.md`'s recorded baseline (2026-06-27 measurement), or explicitly note which run produced the 80% figure and reconcile with the existing doc.
findings.finding: The plan's coverage claim of '~37% vs the true 80%' contradicts the repo's own documented baseline. `docs/coverage.md` records the measured in-process total as **77%** (2026-06-27), not 80%. This is an empirically-checkable numerical claim in the plan that is refuted by the codebase's own measurement record.
findings.location: Why section / What section: 'reports ~37% vs the true 80%, below `fail_under=70`'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '~37% vs the true 80%' with '~37% vs the true 77%' (matching the docs/coverage.md 2026-06-27 baseline), or add a parenthetical: '(docs/coverage.md records 77%; the 80% figure is from the xdist experimental run)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (by reading `test_reconcile_live_e2e.py`'s skip/guard logic) that live tests genuinely self-skip without Jira creds, and cite the specific guard (e.g., `pytest.skip` call or `_requires_live` decorator) in the plan so the 'self-skip' claim is grounded.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that live integration tests 'self-skip without Jira creds' in the CI integration job, using this to justify the claim that 'the command must not assume that'. However, `test_reconcile_live_e2e.py` is marked `[integration, live]` and the current CI integration command is `pytest -m integration` (no live exclusion). The self-skip behaviour is an empirical claim about internal guard logic that cannot be verified from the plan text alone — no grep result confirms a `pytest.skip` or env-var guard in the live e2e file.
findings.location: What section: 'Integration tier' paragraph — 'In CI's integration job they self-skip without Jira creds, but the command must not assume that.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the `nproc` output from an actual `macos-latest` runner run (or use `$(nproc)` dynamically in the workflow step) to ground the `-n3` claim, rather than asserting the core count without evidence.
findings.finding: The plan states `-n3 (macos arm, 3 vCPU)` but the actual workflow matrix uses `macos-latest` — a label whose vCPU count GitHub has changed across runner generations (was 3 for M1, may differ for M2/M3 arm runners). The plan hardcodes `-n3` without citing the verification step output, creating a risk that the hardcoded value is stale before it lands.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `- run: nproc` step in the workflow and record its output in the plan, OR use `$(nproc)` as the argument to `-n` instead of a hardcoded value (which the plan currently avoids due to the over-subscription risk with `-n auto` — but `$(nproc)` is different from `-n auto` and would be accurate).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `.coverage.*` (and `.coverage`) are listed in `.gitignore`; if not, add them as part of this change so the session-level porcelain backstop in `tests/conftest.py` does not fire on worker coverage files written during xdist runs.
findings.finding: The plan's AC states 'no .coverage.* files left in the checkout' but does not verify that `.coverage.*` is gitignored. With `parallel=true`, coverage.py writes `.coverage.<pid>` worker files during the xdist run; the `tests/conftest.py` session-level porcelain backstop (`pytest_sessionfinish` diffs `git status --porcelain`) will fire on any un-gitignored dirty working-tree entry. If `.coverage.*` is not already in `.gitignore`, the pytest-cov cleanup (which removes worker files after `coverage combine`) may race with the porcelain check, causing a spurious 'REPO ISOLATION FAILURE' even on a passing run — turning the xdist change into a self-defeating red CI.
findings.location: AC bullet 2 ('no .coverage.* files left in the checkout') + Scope ('pyproject.toml … parallel') + tests/conftest.py:pytest_sessionfinish
findings.suggested_fix: Add `.coverage` and `.coverage.*` to `.gitignore` (standard coverage.py gitignore entries) and reference this in the Scope section alongside the `pyproject.toml` changes.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
